title: How AI Learns: You Teach It!
modules: The Machine That Watches and Learns
Feeding an AI: Data Is Its Food
What Happens When Data Is Wrong?
synopsis: Join a cast of kids who accidentally teach a classroom robot some very strange habits, then race to fix it by understanding how machines actually learn from examples. You will make real decisions about what data to feed an AI and see the results immediately in the story.
rationale: Children aged 8-10 interact daily with recommendation systems and smart devices but have no mental model for machine learning, leaving them unable to question why an AI behaves the way it does.
learning_outcome: Students can explain in their own words that AI learns from data examples, demonstrate how bad data leads to bad results, and produce a labeled training set for a simple sorting task.
source_signals: Teachers can now use AI to give feedback to students in Google Classroom - Neowin
Beyond the blackboard: How AI is empowering teachers and students in the classroom - Microsoft Source
Gemini in Classroom: No-cost AI tools that amplify teaching and learning - blog.google
Google's Education AI Tools Reach 10 Million Students in 2025 - The Tech Buzz
Redlands Unified teachers, students to have access to new AI tools through Google partnership
